# EconoPET Hardware Design (Timing Context)

This document describes the EconoPET board-level hardware design with enough
detail for an AI agent to derive SDC timing constraints. It is intended to be
read alongside the gateware source in `gw/EconoPET/src/` and the existing SDC
file at `gw/EconoPET/EconoPET.sdc`.

## FPGA

| Property       | Value                                      |
|----------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Device         | Efinix Trion T8 (T8Q144)                   |
| Package        | QFP-144                                    |
| I/O Standard   | 3.3 V LVTTL / LVCMOS (all banks)          |
| I/O Registers  | None (all GPIOs are unregistered at the pad) |
| Speed Grade    | Default                                    |

### PLL

A single PLL (`PLL_TL1`) generates the system clock:

| Parameter          | Value        |
|--------------------|--------------|
| Reference input    | 18 MHz (from RP2040 PWM on GPIO 15, derived from 12 MHz crystal) |
| PLL multiplier     | 64           |
| PLL pre-divider    | 1            |
| PLL post-divider   | 2            |
| Output divider     | 9            |
| Output frequency   | 64 MHz       |
| Output net         | `sys_clock_i` (routed to global clock network via `gclk`) |
| Feedback mode      | Internal     |

The reference clock is generated by the RP2040's PWM peripheral from the
on-board 12 MHz crystal oscillator (typical accuracy 30 ppm). The PLL's
`is_bypass_lock` is true, meaning the FPGA does not wait for PLL lock before
releasing the core from reset.

## Clock Domains

| Clock         | Frequency | Source          | Domain     | Notes |
|---------------|-----------|-----------------|------------|-------|
| `sys_clock_i` | 64 MHz    | PLL output      | Primary    | All synchronous logic except SPI shift register |
| `spi0_sck_i`  | 24 MHz    | RP2040 SPI0 SCK | SPI        | External clock, center-aligned SDR (mode 0) |

These two clock domains are asynchronous. The SDC declares them with
`set_clock_groups -asynchronous`.

### Derived Clock Enables

The `timing` module divides `sys_clock_i` using a 6-bit counter to produce
clock enables (active for a single `sys_clock_i` cycle):

| Enable      | Effective Rate | Purpose                        |
|-------------|----------------|--------------------------------|
| `clk16_en`  | 16 MHz         | 80-column video pixel clock    |
| `clk8_en`   | 8 MHz          | 40-column video pixel clock, bus slot advance |
| (CPU slot)  | ~1 MHz         | CPU PHI2 cycle (see Bus Arbitration) |

These are not separate clock domains. They are single-cycle strobes
synchronous to `sys_clock_i`.

## Bus Arbitration

The system bus is time-division multiplexed into 8 slots of 8 `sys_clock_i`
cycles each (one 64-cycle frame). The `timing` module assigns slots as
follows:

| Slot | Grant      | Purpose                                |
|------|------------|----------------------------------------|
| 0    | VIDEO_1    | Video VRAM/VROM fetch (even character) |
| 1    | VIDEO_2    | Video VROM fetch (even pixels)         |
| 2    | SPI_1      | SPI/Wishbone bridge access             |
| 3    | VIDEO_3    | Video VRAM fetch (odd character, 80-col only) |
| 4    | VIDEO_4    | Video VROM fetch (odd pixels, 80-col only) |
| 5    | SPI_2      | SPI/Wishbone bridge access             |
| 6    | CPU_1      | CPU bus cycle (first half)             |
| 7    | CPU_2      | CPU bus cycle (second half)            |

During CPU slots (6-7), the FPGA deasserts its bus drivers and the CPU owns
the address and data buses. During non-CPU slots, the FPGA can drive the buses
for SPI bridge or video fetch operations.

The Wishbone bus mux (`wbc_mux`) selects between the video controller and SPI
bridge as bus controllers based on the current grant.

## External Components and Timing Parameters

### W65C02S CPU

| Parameter | Symbol | Min  | Max  | Units |
|-----------|--------|------|------|-------|
| Cycle time | tCYC  | 125  | -    | ns    |
| Clock pulse width high | tPWH | 62 | - | ns |
| Clock pulse width low | tPWL | 63 | - | ns |
| BE to valid data | tBVD | - | 30 | ns |
| Address setup time | tADS | - | 40 | ns |
| Address hold time | tAH | 10 | - | ns |
| Read data setup time | tDSR | 15 | - | ns |
| Read data hold time | tDHR | 10 | - | ns |
| Write data delay time | tMDS | - | 40 | ns |
| Write data hold time | tDHW | 10 | - | ns |
| Processor control setup | tPCS | 15 | - | ns |
| Processor control hold | tPCH | 10 | - | ns |

The CPU address bus (A0-A15), data bus (D0-D7), PHI2, BE, RDY, RWB, /RES,
/NMI, and /IRQ connect directly to FPGA I/O pins (no transceiver). The FPGA
generates PHI2 (cpu_clock_o), BE (cpu_be_o), and RDY (cpu_ready_o). /RES and
/NMI are active-low open-drain, driven via output-enable on the FPGA side.

### AS6C1008-55PCN SRAM (128KB)

| Parameter | Symbol | Min | Max | Units |
|-----------|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| Read cycle time | tRC | 55 | - | ns |
| Address access time | tAA | - | 55 | ns |
| OE access time | tOE | - | 30 | ns |
| OE to output low-Z | tOLZ | - | 5 | ns |
| OE disable to high-Z | tOHZ | - | 20 | ns |
| Output hold from addr change | tOH | 10 | - | ns |
| Write cycle time | tWC | 55 | - | ns |
| Address valid to end of write | tAW | 50 | - | ns |
| Address setup time | tAS | 0 | - | ns |
| Write pulse width | tWP | 45 | - | ns |
| Write recovery time | tWR | 0 | - | ns |
| Data to write overlap | tDW | 25 | - | ns |
| Data hold from end of write | tDH | 0 | - | ns |

#### SRAM Address Bus Wiring

The SRAM address bus is wired as follows:

- **A0-A9, A12-A14**: Connected directly from the CPU address bus to the SRAM.
  These bits are shared (physically wired CPU-to-SRAM). The FPGA can also
  drive them via `cpu_addr_o` when `cpu_addr_oe` is asserted (i.e., when
  `cpu_be_o` is deasserted).
- **A10, A11**: Actively muxed by the FPGA (`ram_addr_a10_o`, `ram_addr_a11_o`).
  When the CPU owns the bus, the FPGA applies video RAM address masking. When
  the FPGA owns the bus, it drives the Wishbone bridge address.
- **A15**: Muxed by the FPGA (`ram_addr_a15_o`) for 64KB RAM expansion bank
  selection.
- **A16**: Muxed by the FPGA (`ram_addr_a16_o`) for RAM expansion bank
  selection.

#### SRAM Data Bus Wiring

The SRAM data bus (D0-D7) is a shared bus connecting the CPU, FPGA, and SRAM.
At most one device drives the bus at any time:

- **CPU reads from SRAM**: SRAM drives data, CPU latches on PHI2 rising edge.
- **CPU writes to SRAM**: CPU drives data, SRAM latches on WE deassertion.
- **FPGA reads from SRAM**: FPGA asserts OE and reads `cpu_data_i`.
- **FPGA writes to SRAM**: FPGA drives `cpu_data_o` with `cpu_data_oe` asserted.

#### SRAM Control Signals

- CE is active (active-low directly to SRAM or active). Permanently asserted
  (active low directly tied low on board or active high directly tied high).
- OE (`ram_oe_n_o`) and WE (`ram_we_n_o`) are driven by the FPGA.

### SN74LVC4245A Level-Shifting Transceivers

Two SN74LVC4245A devices sit between the shared CPU/SRAM bus and the PIA/VIA
I/O chips:

- **Address transceiver**: CPU address A0-A3 to PIA/VIA (fixed direction
  B-to-A, permanently enabled).
- **Data transceiver**: CPU data D0-D7 to PIA/VIA (bidirectional, DIR follows
  CPU RWB). /OE is controlled by the FPGA (`io_oe_n_o`) and only asserted
  during decoded PIA/VIA accesses.

| Parameter | Symbol | Min | Max | Units |
|-----------|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| Propagation A-to-B | tPLH | 1 | 6.7 | ns |
| Propagation B-to-A | tPHL | 1 | 6.1 | ns |
| Enable to output low-Z (B) | tPZL | 1 | 10.3 | ns |
| Enable to output high-Z (B) | tPHZ | 1 | 7.8 | ns |

The FPGA's `io_oe_n_o` output must account for the transceiver's enable delay
(up to 10.3 ns) when budgeting setup time for PIA/VIA reads.

### W65C21N PIA (PIA1, PIA2)

| Parameter | Symbol | Min | Max | Units |
|-----------|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| PHI2 cycle time | tCYC | 70 | - | ns |
| PHI2 pulse width | tC | 35 | - | ns |
| Address setup (read) | tACR | 8 | - | ns |
| Address hold (read) | tCAR | 0 | - | ns |
| Data bus delay | tCDR | - | 20 | ns |
| Data bus hold | tHR | 5 | - | ns |
| Address setup (write) | tACW | 8 | - | ns |
| Address hold (write) | tCAW | 0 | - | ns |
| Data bus setup (write) | tDCW | 10 | - | ns |
| Data bus hold (write) | tHW | 5 | - | ns |

### W65C22N VIA

| Parameter | Symbol | Min | Max | Units |
|-----------|--------|-----|-----|-------|
| Cycle time | tCYC | 70 | - | ns |
| PHI2 pulse width high | tPWH | 35 | - | ns |
| PHI2 pulse width low | tPWL | 35 | - | ns |
| CSx/RSx/RWB setup (read) | tACR | 10 | - | ns |
| CSx/RSx/RWB hold (read) | tCAR | 10 | - | ns |
| Data bus delay | tCDR | - | 20 | ns |
| Data bus hold | tHR | 10 | - | ns |
| CSx/RSx/RWB setup (write) | tACW | 10 | - | ns |
| CSx/RSx/RWB hold (write) | tCAW | 10 | - | ns |
| Data bus setup (write) | tDCW | 10 | - | ns |
| Data bus hold (write) | tHW | 10 | - | ns |

## PCB Trace Delays

All traces are under 100 mm except where noted. At a typical propagation
velocity of ~6 ns/m (FR-4 microstrip), trace delays are estimated as:

| Signal          | Trace Length | Estimated Delay |
|-----------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Most signals    | < 100 mm     | < 0.6 ns        |
| CPU PHI2        | ~120 mm      | ~0.7 ns         |
| CPU RWB         | ~150 mm      | ~0.9 ns         |
| CPU /IRQ        | ~230 mm      | ~1.4 ns         |
| CPU /NMI        | ~250 mm      | ~1.5 ns         |
| CPU /RES        | ~250 mm      | ~1.5 ns         |

For timing constraint purposes, a conservative board delay budget of 2 ns
covers all signals.

## SPI Interface

The SPI bus connects the RP2040 MCU to the FPGA:

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| SPI mode | 0 (CPOL=0, CPHA=0) |
| Max SCK frequency | 24 MHz (41.67 ns period) |
| Data sampling | Rising SCK edge |
| Data shifting | Falling SCK edge |
| Framing | CS_N (manually toggled by MCU firmware) |

### Clock Domain Crossing

The SPI module (`spi.sv`) operates in the `spi0_sck_i` clock domain. The
`spi1_controller` module bridges SPI to the Wishbone bus using 2-stage
synchronizers (`sync2` with `async_reg` attribute) for:

- `spi_cs_ni` (CS_N assertion/deassertion edges)
- `spi_strobe` (byte-complete pulse)

The synchronizers introduce 2-3 `sys_clock_i` cycle latency (31-47 ns at
64 MHz).

### Flow Control

The FPGA asserts `spi_stall_o` while processing a command. The MCU must poll
this signal before starting a new SPI transaction.

## FPGA Output Signal Mapping

### Directly Registered Outputs

All of the following output signals are driven by `always_ff` blocks clocked
on `posedge sys_clock_i` (registered in fabric, but the I/O pad itself has no
output register):

| Port              | Driven By        | Notes |
|-------------------|------------------|-------|
| `cpu_clock_o`     | `timing`         | PHI2 clock to CPU |
| `cpu_be_o`        | `timing`         | Bus enable to CPU |
| `cpu_ready_o`     | `register_file`  | RDY to CPU |
| `cpu_reset_n_oe`  | `register_file`  | Open-drain /RES |
| `cpu_nmi_n_oe`    | `register_file`  | Open-drain /NMI |
| `ram_oe_n_o`      | combinational    | SRAM output enable (derived from `cpu_be_o`, `ram_en`, `ram_ctl_oe`) |
| `ram_we_n_o`      | combinational    | SRAM write enable (derived from `cpu_be_o`, `cpu_we_i`, `ram_en`, `ram_ctl_we`) |
| `io_oe_n_o`       | combinational    | I/O transceiver enable |
| `pia1_cs_n_o`     | combinational    | PIA1 chip select |
| `pia2_cs_n_o`     | combinational    | PIA2 chip select |
| `via_cs_n_o`      | combinational    | VIA chip select |
| `ram_addr_a10_o`  | combinational    | Muxed RAM A10 |
| `ram_addr_a11_o`  | combinational    | Muxed RAM A11 |
| `ram_addr_a15_o`  | combinational    | Muxed RAM A15 |
| `ram_addr_a16_o`  | combinational    | Muxed RAM A16 |
| `cpu_addr_o[15:0]`| registered       | From `ram.sv` address capture |
| `cpu_data_o[7:0]` | registered       | From `cpu_data_mux` |
| `cpu_we_n_o`      | tied low (0)     | Currently unused |
| `h_sync_o`        | registered       | Video horizontal sync |
| `v_sync_o`        | registered       | Video vertical sync |
| `video_o`         | registered       | Video data |
| `audio_o`         | registered       | Audio output |
| `spi0_sd_o`       | `spi.sv`         | SPI data out (SCK domain) |
| `spi_stall_o`     | registered       | SPI flow control |

### Combinational Output Paths

The following outputs are driven by combinational logic from `sys_clock_i`
domain flip-flops. These have longer output delays due to LUT propagation:

- `ram_oe_n_o`: depends on `cpu_be_o`, `cpu_we_i` (input), `ram_en` (from
  address decoding), `ram_ctl_oe` (from Wishbone RAM peripheral)
- `ram_we_n_o`: depends on `cpu_be_o`, `cpu_we_i` (input), `cpu_clock_o`,
  `ram_en`, `is_readonly`, `ram_ctl_we`
- `io_oe_n_o`, `pia1_cs_n_o`, `pia2_cs_n_o`, `via_cs_n_o`: depend on
  address decoding of `cpu_addr_i` and `cpu_be_o`
- `ram_addr_a10_o`, `ram_addr_a11_o`, `ram_addr_a15_o`, `ram_addr_a16_o`:
  muxed between CPU-driven and FPGA-driven values based on `cpu_be_o`

## CPU Bus Cycle Timing

The `timing` module generates the CPU bus cycle within slots 6-7 of the
64-cycle frame. The cycle counts (in `sys_clock_i` cycles from the start of
the CPU slot) are computed from component timing parameters:

| Event              | Cycle Offset | Purpose |
|--------------------|-------------|---------|
| `CPU_BE_START`     | 0           | Assert BE (CPU takes ownership of bus) |
| `CPU_PHI_START`    | +4          | Assert PHI2 (after tBVD + tIOTX delays) |
| `CPU_DATA_STROBE`  | +7          | Capture read data / validate write data |
| `CPU_PHI_END`      | +9          | Deassert PHI2 (after tPWH minimum met) |
| `CPU_BE_END`       | +11         | Deassert BE (after tDHx hold time met) |

The CPU effective clock rate is 1 MHz (one PHI2 cycle per 64 `sys_clock_i`
cycles, i.e., 64 / 64 MHz = 1 us).

## Wishbone Bus

The internal Wishbone B4 pipelined bus connects:

**Controllers:**
- SPI bridge (`spi1_controller`): MCU read/write access via SPI
- Video controller (`video`): VRAM and character ROM fetches

**Peripherals:**
- RAM (`ram`): External SRAM read/write with multi-cycle state machine
- Register file (`register_file`): CPU control, status, video config
- Keyboard (`keyboard`): USB keyboard matrix state
- CRTC (`video_crtc`): CRT controller register access
- BRAM (`bram`): On-chip character ROM (4KB)

Address decoding is combinational from `wb_addr` in `main.sv`.

## Key Timing Paths for Constraint Derivation

### 1. sys_clock_i Internal Paths

All core logic runs on one clock. Setup/hold analysis is straightforward at
15.625 ns period. The most timing-critical internal paths are likely:

- Address decoding logic (combinational `casez` in `main.sv` and
  `address_decoding.sv`)
- Wishbone peripheral mux (`wbp_mux`) and controller mux (`wbc_mux`)
- CPU data mux (`cpu_data_mux`) with 4 sources

### 2. SPI I/O Paths (spi0_sck_i domain)

- **Input (spi0_sd_i)**: Sampled on rising SCK, transitions on falling SCK.
  Constrained with `set_input_delay` relative to falling SCK edge.
- **Output (spi0_sd_o)**: Shifted out on falling SCK. Constrained with
  `set_output_delay` relative to falling SCK edge.
- **CS_N (spi0_cs_ni)**: Used as async reset in `spi.sv`. Currently a false
  path in the SDC.

### 3. CPU-Facing Output Paths (sys_clock_i to pad)

These are constrained with `set_output_delay` relative to `sys_clock_i`:

- `cpu_clock_o`, `cpu_be_o`, `cpu_ready_o`: Registered outputs.
- `ram_oe_n_o`, `ram_we_n_o`: Combinational from registered signals. The
  relationship between address output delay and WE assertion is critical (WE
  minimum delay must be >= maximum address output delay to maintain SRAM
  address setup to end-of-write).
- `cpu_data_o`, `cpu_addr_o`: Directly driven for FPGA bus master operations.

### 4. CPU-Facing Input Paths (pad to sys_clock_i)

- `cpu_addr_i[15:0]`: Sampled during CPU slots for address decoding and SRAM
  address mux. Not currently constrained with `set_input_delay` in the SDC
  (the CPU drives addresses synchronous to PHI2, which the FPGA generates).
- `cpu_data_i[7:0]`: Sampled at `cpu_data_strobe` timing point. Same
  consideration as address.
- `cpu_we_n_i`: Sampled for read/write direction.

These inputs are quasi-static relative to `sys_clock_i` because the CPU's
timing is controlled by FPGA-generated PHI2. The data is guaranteed valid by
the time the FPGA samples it (multiple `sys_clock_i` cycles after the relevant
edge). This may justify `set_false_path` or relaxed `set_input_delay`
constraints rather than full-speed setup/hold analysis.

### 5. Asynchronous Inputs

The following inputs are asynchronous and should be false-pathed or
constrained with `set_max_delay`:

- `config_crt_i`, `config_keyboard_i`: DIP switch settings (static)
- `graphic_i`: VIA CA2 output (asynchronous to sys_clock_i)
- `diag_i`, `via_cb2_i`: Audio-related inputs
- `cpu_reset_n_i`, `cpu_irq_n_i`, `cpu_nmi_n_i`: Active-low open-drain signals
